description: Fix validation gaps in shared URL pattern validation and add size limits to plugin SDK log serialization.

userStories.title: Reject wildcard-host URL patterns like https://*/*
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want overly broad URL patterns with wildcard hosts to be rejected so that plugins cannot accidentally inject adapters into every website.
acceptanceCriteria: validateUrlPattern rejects 'https://*/*' with a clear error message about the host being too broad
validateUrlPattern rejects 'http://*/*' with the same error
validateUrlPattern still accepts 'https://*/api' — a wildcard host with a restrictive path is allowed (existing test at line 189)

Wait — re-check: 'https://*/api' currently passes. The fix should reject host='*' entirely when the path is also a wildcard (/*). Actually, host='*' is inherently too broad regardless of path. Reject any pattern where host is exactly '*'.
validateUrlPattern rejects any pattern where the host component is exactly '*' (bare wildcard host)
Existing patterns like '*://*.example.com/*' (wildcard subdomain) still pass
Existing patterns like '*://example.com/*' (specific domain) still pass
Unit tests in platform/shared/src/validation.test.ts cover the new rejection cases
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add size limit to log argument serialization
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want log entry serialization to be bounded so that a single log call with a huge object does not produce a multi-megabyte message over WebSocket.
acceptanceCriteria: safeSerializeArg truncates JSON-serialized output to a maximum size (e.g., 64KB or similar reasonable limit)
When truncation occurs, the output includes a marker indicating it was truncated (e.g., appending '...[truncated]')
Small objects (under the limit) are serialized unchanged
The MAX_STRING_LENGTH constant (4096) for individual string arguments is preserved
The MAX_DATA_LENGTH constant (10) for top-level argument count is preserved
Unit test verifies that a large nested object is truncated to within the size limit
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
